steps.title: Identify the barrier to the ring-counting method
steps.content: The researcher claims the ring-counting method “often can't be used” for olive trees. The passage describes how the method works: look at a tree stump and count the dark rings. For the method to fail, something must prevent you from seeing and counting the rings.

steps.title: Match the barrier to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C directly explains why the method can't be used: if the growth rings are difficult to see, you can't count them accurately, so the method becomes unusable.

The other choices discuss tree longevity (A), the meaning of narrow rings (B), or climate conditions (D)—none of which explain why the ring-counting method itself fails.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
